are not in a fit shape to run their
cars after they have been at work
over 10 hours. Several of the acci-
dents in the past have been due to the
fact that men were sleepy and
drowsy from overwork.


The split run which we want is in
force on the lines of the Spokane
Traction company. 1t Is also in force
in Portland and on some of the runs
in Seattle.


THE assertion has been made that
the employees of the Washington
Water Power company are drawing
from 20 to 80 cents an hour. AS a
matter of fact there are two men
drawing 80 cents an hour. Not moro
than seven are drawing over rs cents
an hour. The most Of us are paid
from 20 to 26 cents an hour"


The men say they have been given
to understand that unless they keep
their mouths closed, they, too, will
feel the axe.


This plan of reprisals on the conn.,
pany's part has caused great indigna-
ton among the men, who are now
organizing and intend to make more
demands.


A committee of employees called at
The Press office today and said that
within a few days request for an
increase in wages and shorter hours
will be made.


The committee requested that their
names be withheld for fear the pub
LEGATION would result in more dis
charges.


The present scale of wages Is 20
cents per hour for the hrst SIX
months; 22 cents for the next year:
20 cents for the next year; rs cents
after being in the employe of the
company for five years; p cents after
10 years service and 80 years after
is years of continuous service.
